justification: All pass criteria met: 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Fleet Management, Delivery Management, Customer Notification). Cross-context dependencies explicitly identified (vehicle class spanning Fleet and Delivery, recalculation triggers spanning Route Planning and Delivery Management, notification window spanning Route Planning and Customer Notification). Vehicle class rule correctly marked invariant:true (BR-1), stop duration correctly marked invariant:false (BR-8), notification window correctly marked invariant:false (BR-7). 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). All three recalculation triggers enumerated (new delivery, failed delivery, driver deviation) in BR-4/5/6.

observations.text: Identified 4 distinct bounded contexts with clear ownership boundaries — exceeds the 3 minimum
Cross-context dependencies surfaced explicitly, including vehicle class spanning Fleet and Delivery contexts, and recalculation triggers crossing Route Planning and Delivery Management boundaries

Invariant classification is precise — vehicle class (true), zone constraint (true), failed delivery removal (true), while recalculation triggers, notification window, and stop duration correctly marked false with strong justifications
10 acceptance criteria with concrete scenarios including edge cases like no-compatible-deliveries (AC-8), duplicate notification prevention (AC-9), and multi-driver assignment (AC-10)
Excellent edge case identifying active vehicle vs licensed vehicle class distinction — a genuine domain insight that goes beyond the spec
Escalations are well-structured with concrete options and recommendations, surfacing real ambiguities for MJ/stakeholders

Route recalculation triggers (BR-4/5/6) marked invariant:false — the rubric expected invariant:true for these. Bird's justification is defensible (they are optimization policies, not data integrity constraints) but diverges from the expected answer
BR-9 (failed delivery removal from route) correctly identified as a separate invariant from the recalculation policy, showing nuanced domain thinking
Rich ubiquitous language section with 12 well-defined terms including subtle distinctions like Licensed Vehicle Class vs Vehicle Class
